MONET 0.1.0
  • Reference

Vignette Individual Relatedness Matrix (M)

Source: R/data.R
vignette_The_M.Rd

A symmetric block-diagonal matrix containing within-population relatedness coefficients for F1 individuals. This matrix describes the kinship between pairs of individuals within populations, adjusted for population structure.

data(vignette_The_M)

Format

A 900x900 numeric matrix where:

Diagonal elements

Self-relatedness (typically 1)

Within-population blocks

Adjusted relatedness between individuals from the same population

Between-population elements

Zero (no relatedness between populations)

Source

Calculated from simulated F1 neutral marker data using hierfstat::beta.dosage()

Details

The M matrix represents the kinship-based relatedness among F1 individuals, adjusted to account for the population structure captured in Theta.P. The adjustment is: M_x = relatedness_x × (1 - Theta.P_xx)

This matrix has a block-diagonal structure where:

  • Each block corresponds to one population (50×50 for this dataset)

  • Within blocks: non-zero values representing family structure

  • Between blocks: zeros (individuals from different populations)

The matrix is used as input to the monet() function to model within-population genetic structure and family relationships.

See also

calculate_coancestries for computing M from genetic data or pedigree

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Load the data
M <- readRDS(
  system.file("extdata", "vignette_The_M.rds", package = "MONET")
)

# Check dimensions
dim(M)

# Verify block-diagonal structure
image(M[1:100, 1:100])  # First two populations

# Check eigenvalues (should be positive semi-definite)
min(eigen(M)$values)
} # }

Contents
